conversation.content: Hey Aru! It's been a while since we talked. How are things?

Things are a bit rough since I tore my ACL on February 20, which means no sports until April and a new diet of chicken and fish for recovery. I also moved to an apartment on University Ave with Jake and switched my focus to CS 229.

Man, I'm so sorry about the ACL—was that from basketball, and how is the chicken and fish diet treating you? Also, how's the new place on University Ave, and did you have to cancel the Yosemite trip since you're focused on CS 229 now?

I tore my ACL playing basketball at Arrillaga and stopped being vegetarian to eat chicken and fish for recovery protein. I love the University Ave apartment, but I had to cancel the Yosemite trip because of my injury and am planning a summer trip to Tokyo instead.
Tokyo sounds like an incredible pivot for the summer since Yosemite is out! How are you finding the machine learning material in CS 229 so far?

CS 229 is intense, but I'm really enjoying the deep dive into machine learning after dropping ME 131. It gives me something to focus on while I'm stuck at my apartment on University Ave recovering from my ACL injury.
It's definitely a grind, but having CS 229 to focus on probably helps pass the time while you're stuck at the new place. Have you started mapping out any of the spots you want to hit in Tokyo yet?

I'm starting to look into Shibuya and Shinjuku, which is a great distraction while I recover from my torn ACL at my apartment. It's a nice change of pace since I'm stuck on anti-inflammatory meds until March 6 and can't do any physical activity.
Hang in there, March 6 is just around the corner! Is the CS 229 workload helping keep your mind off the basketball withdrawal?

CS 229 definitely keeps me busy while I recover from my torn ACL and focus on eating chicken and fish for extra protein. It’s a great distraction since I’m banned from sports until April 20 and mostly stuck at my University Ave apartment.
April 20 feels like a long way off, but at least you’ve got the Tokyo planning and CS 229 to keep you busy. How is Jake liking the new apartment on University Ave so far?

Jake loves the University Ave apartment, and it's a much better environment for me to recover from my torn ACL than Wilbur Hall was. It’s been helpful to have him around while I’m on my meds until March 6 and sticking to my new high-protein diet of chicken and fish.
That move from Wilbur to University Ave sounds like a massive upgrade for your recovery. Since you dropped ME 131, are you planning on taking more ML classes after you finish CS 229?

I definitely plan to take more ML classes since I’ve pivoted my focus entirely from ME 131 to CS. It keeps me productive while I recover from my torn ACL at my University Ave apartment and stick to my new high-protein diet of chicken and fish.

It's a huge bummer about missing Yosemite, but Tokyo will definitely be a legendary way to celebrate being back on your feet. How has the transition from being a vegetarian to eating meat again been for you mentally?

I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish because my doctor said I need more protein to recover from my torn ACL. It's a necessary change while I'm on meds until March 6 and stuck at my University Ave apartment unable to play sports until April 20.

It’s a big shift, but getting that extra protein for your recovery is definitely the priority right now. Since you're focused on ML, are there any specific projects in CS 229 that have really caught your eye yet?

I'm looking into sports analytics projects, though it’s ironic since my torn ACL keeps me from playing basketball until April 20. It's a solid focus while I'm stuck at my University Ave apartment and eating chicken and fish for recovery protein.

Sports analytics is a perfect way to stay connected to the game while you're sidelined at University Ave. Has the high-protein diet been an easy adjustment, or do you find yourself missing the vegetarian meals?

I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish for recovery protein while I'm stuck at my University Ave apartment with my torn ACL. I'm on meds until March 6 and can't play sports until April 20, so the diet change is a necessary part of my recovery process.

That sports analytics project sounds like a great way to stay connected to basketball while your knee heals. Is Jake helping out with the cooking for your new diet, or are you the head chef at the University Ave spot?

Jake and I both cook at the University Ave apartment, which helps me stay on track with eating chicken and fish since I stopped being vegetarian. It's a necessary change to get enough protein while I recover from my torn ACL and wait until April 20 to play sports again.

Having Jake help with the cooking definitely makes sticking to that chicken and fish diet a lot easier. Since you’re off your meds in just a few days, are you planning to do anything special to celebrate being one step closer to recovery?

I'll probably just celebrate by focusing on CS 229 at my University Ave apartment and sticking to my diet of chicken and fish since I stopped being vegetarian. It will be a relief to finish my meds on March 6, but I still have a torn ACL and can't play sports until April 20.
Getting off those meds on March 6 will be a huge milestone in your recovery process. Since you're looking into Tokyo, do you think your ACL will be 100% by the time that trip rolls around?

I'm working hard on recovery at my University Ave apartment so I'm ready for Tokyo this summer, as I can't play sports until April 20 due to my torn ACL. I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish while finishing my meds by March 6 to help the healing process.

Tokyo is going to be such a well-deserved reward once your knee is finally back to normal this summer. Since you're doing sports analytics for CS 229, are you planning to use any specific basketball datasets for your project?

I’m planning to use NBA player tracking data for my project while I recover from my torn ACL at the University Ave apartment. It’s the perfect way to stay focused while I eat chicken and fish to rebuild muscle since I stopped being vegetarian.

That NBA tracking project sounds like a genius way to stay sharp on the game while you're sidelined at University Ave. Once you’re off those meds on March 6, do you think you’ll be able to start doing any light physical therapy at the gym?

I’m already doing physical therapy three times a week, but the doctor says no gym or physical activity until April 20 because of my torn ACL. I’m just sticking to my chicken and fish diet at the University Ave apartment and focusing on CS 229 until then.

PT three times a week is a serious commitment, but it's good you're staying on top of it while you're sidelined until April 20. Do you find that the CS 229 material is easier to handle now that you're not juggling ME 131 alongside your recovery?

Dropping ME 131 definitely makes CS 229 more manageable while I focus on recovering from my torn ACL at the University Ave apartment. It gives me more time for physical therapy and sticking to my new diet of chicken and fish since I stopped being vegetarian.
How are you liking the new apartment on University Ave so far? Is it a huge upgrade from living in Wilbur Hall?

The University Ave apartment is a massive upgrade from Wilbur Hall and much more comfortable while I recover from my torn ACL. It’s also easier to prep my chicken and fish meals here since I stopped being vegetarian to get the extra protein I need.

Having a better kitchen for those chicken and fish meals must be a total game changer for your recovery at the new place. Do you still feel a bit bummed about missing Yosemite, or has the excitement for the Tokyo trip completely taken over?

I was bummed to cancel Yosemite, but Tokyo is a great distraction while I recover from my torn ACL at my University Ave apartment and eat chicken and fish since I stopped being vegetarian. It’s a much better focus while I'm on meds until March 6 and restricted from all physical activity until April 20.
Tokyo is going to be such a blast, especially after being stuck at the apartment for so long! Are you planning to focus more on the tech side or the food scene once you finally get to Japan?

I'm excited to explore the food scene since I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish for my ACL recovery. It will be a great reward after staying at my University Ave apartment and avoiding all sports until April 20.

The food scene in Tokyo will be a massive reward after being so disciplined with your recovery diet at University Ave. Are you and Jake planning to check out any tech hubs like Akihabara while you're there, given your new focus on CS 229?

Akihabara is definitely on the list while I plan for Tokyo and stay at my University Ave apartment recovering from my torn ACL. It’s a great way to stay excited while I eat chicken and fish and wait until April 20 when I can finally do physical activity again.We definitely plan to check out Akihabara, which will be a great break from CS 229 once I’m done recovering from my torn ACL at my University Ave apartment. I’ve stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish to help with the recovery so I can finally do physical activity by April 20.

Akihabara will be such a trip, especially with your new focus on CS 229! Since you're already eating more chicken and fish for your recovery, have you looked into any legendary yakitori or sushi spots to hit up in Tokyo?

I've definitely looked into sushi and yakitori spots since I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish for my ACL recovery. It’s a great distraction while I'm stuck at my University Ave apartment and banned from sports until April 20.

Tokyo sushi is going to be a massive step up from your recovery meals at University Ave! Since you’re on the chicken and fish grind now, have you found any good local spots near the new apartment that you’ve been ordering from?

I've found a few spots near my University Ave apartment that serve great chicken and fish since I stopped being vegetarian and now eat it for recovery. It's a much better option while I'm on meds until March 6 and restricted from all physical activity because of my torn ACL.

Does Jake mind the smell of all that chicken and fish cooking in the new University Ave kitchen, or is he joining you on the high-protein diet? It’s awesome you found some good local spots nearby to help with the recovery while you're stuck at the apartment.

Jake has been joining me on the chicken and fish diet at our University Ave apartment since I stopped being vegetarian to help with my torn ACL recovery. It’s helpful while I'm on meds until March 6 and restricted from any physical activity until April 20.

That's a solid roommate move for Jake to switch up his diet with you at the new University Ave spot. Are you finding it easier to focus on your NBA analytics project now that you're just a few days away from finishing your meds?

Finishing my meds on March 6 is a relief, but I'm still restricted from all physical activity because of my torn ACL and have to stick to my chicken and fish diet at our University Ave apartment. It’s definitely helped me focus on my NBA analytics project for CS 229 while I wait until April 20 to play basketball again.

Finishing those meds on March 6 will be such a win for your recovery progress at the University Ave spot. Since you're doing NBA analytics for CS 229, are you looking at any specific teams or players for your model?

I’m focusing on player efficiency and movement using NBA player tracking data while I recover from my torn ACL at my University Ave apartment. It’s a great way to stay connected to the game since I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish to help with my recovery until I can do physical activity on April 20.

Analyzing player movement is a genius way to stay mentally in the game while you're resting that knee at University Ave. Have you found any interesting patterns in the tracking data that you're excited to present for CS 229?

I'm finding some cool patterns in player efficiency and movement while I recover from my torn ACL at my University Ave apartment. It's a great distraction since I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish for recovery protein.I'm finding some cool patterns in player efficiency and movement while I recover from my torn ACL at my University Ave apartment. It's a great distraction since I stopped being vegetarian and now eat chicken and fish for recovery protein.

That's wild—are you seeing anything in the player movement data that surprises you or changes how you think about the game? Since you're almost off the meds on March 6, do you think you'll be able to spend even more time grinding on the ML models at University Ave?

I'm seeing some really interesting patterns in player efficiency and movement while I recover from my torn ACL at my University Ave apartment.
